450144.name: Coal Consumption - TWh
450144.description: Includes commercial solid fuels only, i.e. bituminous coal and anthracite (hard coal), and lignite and brown (sub-bituminous) coal, and other commercial solid fuels. Excludes coal converted to liquid or gaseous fuels, but includes coal consumed in transformation processes. Differences between the consumption figures and the world production statistics are accounted for by stock changes, and unadvoidable disparities in the definition, measurement or conversion of coal supply and demand data.
450144.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450159.name: Gas Consumption - TWh
450159.description: Excludes natural gas converted to liquid fuels but includes derivatives of coal as well as natural gas consumed in Gas-to-Liquids transformation. The difference between the world consumption figures and the world production statistics is due to variations in stocks at storage facilities and liquefaction plants, together with unavoidable disparities in the definition, measurement or conversion of gas supply and demand data.
450159.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450166.name: Geo Biomass Other - TWh
450166.description: Figures are based on gross generation and do not account for cross-border electricity supply.
450166.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450170.name: Hydro Consumption - TWh
450170.description: Figures are based on gross generation and do not account for cross-border electricity supply. "Input-equivalent" energy is the amount of fuel that would be required by thermal power stations to generate the reported electricity output.
450170.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450175.name: Nuclear Consumption - TWh

450175.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450186.name: Oil Consumption - TWh
450186.description: Includes inland demand plus international aviation and marine bunkers and refinery fuel and loss. Consumption of biogasoline (such as ethanol) and biodiesel are excluded while derivatives of coal and natural gas are included. Differences between the world consumption figures and world production statistics are accounted for by stock changes, consumption of non-petroleum additives and substitute fuels and unavoidable disparities in the definition, measurement or conversion of oil supply and demand data.
450186.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

source.name: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)

450204.datasetName: Statistical Review of World Energy - BP (2022)
